stem: A IT operations data backup uses 3 parallel storage devices for redundancy. The data recoverable requires at least one storage device to function. storage device 1 has a reliability of 81%. storage device 2 has a reliability between 76% and 92% (uncertain due to limited operational data). storage device 3 has a reliability of 92%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all storage devices simultaneously, estimated between 4.6% and 8.3%. What is the overall probability that the data recoverable?

ground_truth.answer: P(data recoverable) ranges from 0.9137 to 0.9529. Point estimate: 0.9328.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 storage devices fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.19 × 0.24 × 0.08 = 0.0036. P(works) = 0.9964.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.19 × 0.08 × 0.08 = 0.0012. P(works) = 0.9988.
With common-cause failure (4.6%-8.3%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9137, 0.9529].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
